positive_description: Subtle jaw and lower face adjustment — a very mild forward projection of the chin and slight compression of the lower lip region. The mentalis area tightens. The overall effect is a barely-perceptible jutting or firming of the lower face, giving a quality of quiet determination or stubbornness.
negative_description: Subtle chin retraction and lower lip relaxation. The mentalis area softens and the chin pulls slightly backward. The lower face takes on a marginally weaker, more receding quality.
symmetry_notes: Appears bilaterally symmetric. The 3/4 views show consistent deformation on both sides. No detectable left-right asymmetry.
artifact_notes: No mesh artifacts at +/-3.0. Very subtle component. Generous safe range.
primary_face_region: chin, mentalis, lower lip area, jaw

interactions.psi0: psi0 drives jaw drop with smile character; psi16 adjusts chin projection without opening the mouth
interactions.psi4: psi4 modulates lip pucker/engagement; psi16 operates on the chin/mentalis below the lips

prose: Psi16 is a symmetric lower-face modulator centered on the chin and mentalis region. Its positive pole pushes the chin subtly forward and tightens the mentalis, creating a quality of quiet determination — the jaw firms, the lower face becomes slightly more assertive. Its negative pole does the opposite: the chin recedes marginally and the lower face softens into a weaker posture. This component occupies a functional niche distinct from the primary lower-face drivers (psi0 for jaw drop, psi4 for lip engagement) — it adjusts the structural character of the chin rather than the mouth's expression. At the tested weights, the effects are subtle enough to serve as tonal seasoning rather than expression drivers.
